sublevels.theory:
The **Standard Template Library (STL)** provides ready-made containers:

| Container | Description | Access |
|-----------|-------------|--------|
| `std::vector<T>` | Dynamic array | O(1) random |
| `std::list<T>` | Doubly-linked list | O(n) random |
| `std::map<K,V>` | Sorted key-value (BST) | O(log n) |
| `std::unordered_map<K,V>` | Hash map | O(1) avg |
| `std::set<T>` | Sorted unique values | O(log n) |
| `std::stack<T>` | LIFO adapter | — |
| `std::queue<T>` | FIFO adapter | — |

```cpp
#include <vector>
std::vector<int> v = {1,2,3};
v.push_back(4);
for (int x : v) std::cout << x << ' ';
```

questions.question: Which STL container is a dynamic array?
questions.answer: `std::vector`

questions.question: What is `std::map<K,V>`?
options: Sorted key-value store backed by a balanced BST
questions.answer: Sorted key-value store backed by a balanced BST

questions.question: What is the average time complexity for lookup in `std::unordered_map`?
What follows LIFO order?
questions.answer: `std::stack`

questions.question: What follows FIFO order?
questions.answer: `std::queue`

questions.question: What does `std::vector::push_back(x)` do?
questions.answer: Appends x to end

questions.question: What does `std::set` guarantee about elements?
questions.answer: Sorted and unique

questions.question: How do you access a `std::map` value by key?
questions.answer: Both B and C

questions.question: What does `std::list` offer over `std::vector`?
options: O(1) insertion/deletion anywhere given an iterator
questions.answer: O(1) insertion/deletion anywhere given an iterator

questions.question: Which header is needed for `std::map`?
questions.answer: `<map>`

questions.question: What does `v.begin()` return?
options: An iterator to the first element
questions.answer: An iterator to the first element

questions.question: What does `v.end()` point to?
questions.answer: One past the last element (sentinel)

questions.question: What does `std::sort(v.begin(), v.end())` do?
options: Sorts v in ascending order in-place
questions.answer: Sorts v in ascending order in-place

questions.question: What does `std::find(begin, end, val)` return?
options: Iterator to first occurrence of val, or end if not found
questions.answer: Iterator to first occurrence of val, or end if not found

questions.question: What is `std::priority_queue` by default?
questions.answer: Max-heap

questions.question: What is `std::deque` optimized for?
options: Push/pop at both front and back in O(1)
questions.answer: Push/pop at both front and back in O(1)

questions.question: What does `std::remove(begin, end, val)` do?
options: Moves non-val elements to front, returns new end iterator (erase-remove idiom needs erase() call)
questions.answer: Moves non-val elements to front, returns new end iterator (erase-remove idiom needs erase() call)

questions.question: What does `std::accumulate(begin, end, init)` compute?
options: Cumulative sum (or fold) of elements starting from init
questions.answer: Cumulative sum (or fold) of elements starting from init

questions.question: What additional guarantee does `std::multimap` provide over `std::map`?
options: Allows multiple values for the same key
questions.answer: Allows multiple values for the same key

questions.question: What is the erase-remove idiom?
options: `v.erase(std::remove(begin,end,val), end)` — remove+erase pair for in-place deletion
questions.answer: `v.erase(std::remove(begin,end,val), end)` — remove+erase pair for in-place deletion

sublevels.theory:
**`std::ranges` (C++20):** range-adapted algorithms that avoid iterator pairs:
```cpp
std::ranges::sort(v);
auto sq = v | std::views::transform([](int x){ return x*x; });
```

**`std::flat_map` (C++23):** sorted-array-backed map, better cache performance than tree-based `std::map`.

**`std::span<T>` (C++20):** non-owning view — pass to functions without copying.

**Custom allocators:** STL containers accept an allocator type parameter for fine-grained memory control (e.g., pool allocation).

**`std::pmr::*` (C++17 Polymorphic Memory Resources):** runtime-swappable allocators.

questions.question: What is `std::ranges::sort(v)` compared to `std::sort(v.begin(), v.end())`?
options: Takes the whole range instead of iterator pairs — cleaner, same algorithmic complexity
questions.answer: Takes the whole range instead of iterator pairs — cleaner, same algorithmic complexity

questions.question: What is `std::flat_map` (C++23) and its advantage?
options: A sorted flat (array-based) map with better cache locality than tree-based map
questions.answer: A sorted flat (array-based) map with better cache locality than tree-based map

questions.question: What does a custom allocator in an STL container allow?
options: Fine-grained memory control — e.g., arena/pool allocation, reducing heap fragmentation
questions.answer: Fine-grained memory control — e.g., arena/pool allocation, reducing heap fragmentation

questions.question: What is `std::pmr::vector`?
options: A vector using Polymorphic Memory Resources — allocator selected at runtime
questions.answer: A vector using Polymorphic Memory Resources — allocator selected at runtime

questions.question: What is `std::views::filter | std::views::transform` pipeline?
options: Lazy composition of views that works on demand without creating intermediate containers
questions.answer: Lazy composition of views that works on demand without creating intermediate containers

questions.question: What is the invalidation rule for vector iterators after `push_back`?
questions.answer: Invalidated if reallocation occurs (capacity exceeded)

questions.question: What does `std::unordered_map::reserve(n)` do?
options: Pre-allocates bucket count to avoid rehashing up to n elements
questions.answer: Pre-allocates bucket count to avoid rehashing up to n elements

questions.question: What is node-based vs contiguous container performance trade-off?
options: Node (list/map) has better cache locality; vector has worse
Contiguous (vector/array) has better cache locality; node-based has O(1) stable insert
questions.answer: Contiguous (vector/array) has better cache locality; node-based has O(1) stable insert

questions.question: What is `std::span<T,N>` vs `std::span<T>`?
options: Fixed extent (N known at compile time) vs dynamic extent (runtime size)
N is the max size in both
questions.answer: Fixed extent (N known at compile time) vs dynamic extent (runtime size)

questions.question: What is `std::ranges::views::zip` (C++23)?
options: Creates a lazy view of tuples from multiple ranges, iterating them in lock-step
questions.answer: Creates a lazy view of tuples from multiple ranges, iterating them in lock-step
